Evidence About Contact Disclaimer Swiss site

About this project

About

I am a neurology resident (Assistenzärztin) passionate about the science behind dietary and lifestyle interventions in chronic neurological diseases. This project is my curated map of current evidence, mechanisms, and unanswered questions in ketogenic neurology.

The aim is to make the research readable for clinicians, researchers, patients, and curious families while preserving the boundary between education and individual medical care.

I collate current scientific evidence but do not provide individual medical advice.

Condition pages

Current evidence

Established in selected drug-resistant epilepsy

Epilepsy

The clearest neurological indication for supervised ketogenic dietary therapy.

Read page
Emerging clinical evidence

Alzheimer's

Research asks whether ketones can support brain energy metabolism in cognitive decline.

Read page
Emerging supportive evidence

MS

Diet research in MS is active, but ketogenic diets are not disease-modifying therapy.

Read page
Pilot and feasibility evidence

Parkinson's

A growing research area focused on energy metabolism and non-motor symptoms.

Read page
Adjunctive and investigational

Brain cancers

Metabolic therapy research is active, especially in glioblastoma, but survival benefit is unproven.

Read page
Preclinical and feasibility evidence

Stroke

Ketone biology is promising in models, but stroke care is emergency medicine first.

Read page
Ketoneurology

I collate current scientific evidence but do not provide individual medical advice.
